conditions.today: 6 AM—Get on up, Jackson Hole. You don't want to waste the day because it's the final one of February 2026. Come send it off with a proper blast of big-mountain fun. We'll be dancing under blazing blue skies and a sparkling mirror-ball sun this morning before high afternoon clouds dim the lights down low. Warm temperatures will help tenderize the dance floor as they zoom into the mid-20s at the summit and the mid-40s in the Village. A cool west breeze will blow between 5 and 20 mph on most of the mountain, while the high ridgelines could see gusts that hit the 40 mph mark. After your day of elevated sun-sations in the snow, you don't have to slip down into stagnation. Hit the Tram Dock après show for tunes and libations to sustain your hip gyrations. -Lege
conditions.yesterday: 6 AM—Aww yeah. The stage is set, Jackson. Sunrise is upon us, and the day is dawning in a fantastic fashion. Bluebird skies are welcoming bright solar rays to come spend the day, so grab your gear and join the dramatic alpine play. Come prepared for a springy scene because things will heat up into the mid-20s at the summit and right around 40 in the valley. West winds will cool things off a tad as they blow between 5 and 15 mph at most elevations but gust near 40 at the summit. If life is feeling a bit over the top, just call it a week and head for the Village; your one-stop shop with endless crystal treats to pillage. - Lege
